initial_program:
% -------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Find the colour that has a cell at its
%     bottomright corner (maxRow , maxCol) and
%     also appears on the top row, bottom row,
%     left column and right column of its bounding box.
% -------------------------------------------------
% helper predicates
rowHasC(R,C) :- input(R,_,C).
colHasC(C,R) :- input(_,C,R).

% extremes for each colour
minR(C)  :- R = #min{R : rowHasC(R,C)}.
maxR(C)  :- R = #max{R : rowHasC(R,C)}.
minC(C)  :- C = #min{C : colHasC(C,C)}.
maxC(C)  :- C = #max{C : colHasC(C,C)}.

% corner must contain the colour
corner(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), R = maxR(Col), C = maxC(Col).

% corner existence forces the colour to be used
:- not corner(maxR(K), maxC(K), K).

% choose the highest colour that satisfies the corner condition
maxc(K) :- K = #max{Col : corner(maxR(Col), maxC(Col), Col), color(Col)}.

% interior dimensions (always at least 1)
intRows(K) :- I = maxR(K) - minR(K) - 1.
intCols(K) :- J = maxC(K) - minC(K) - 1.
intRows(K) :- I = max(I,1).      % guarantee at least 1
intCols(K) :- J = max(J,1).      % guarantee at least 1

% output square size (make it a square)
size(S)    :- S = max(I,J).

% centre the interior rectangle inside the square
padTop(S,I)    :- PT = (S - I) \ 2.          % rows added on top
padLeft(S,J)   :- PL = (S - J) \ 2.          % columns added on left

% centre of the plus shape inside the square
centRow(S,I,PT) :- CR = PT + (I - 1) \ 2.   % centre row index
centCol(S,J,PL)  :- CC = PL + (J - 1) \ 2.   % centre column index

% -------------------------------------------------
% 2.  Build the output
% -------------------------------------------------
% rows and columns of the square
row(r) :- r >= 0, r < S.
col(c) :- c >= 0, c < S.

% vertical line (all rows, column = centre column)
output(r,c,Maxc) :- row(r), col(c), c = CC.

% horizontal line (all columns, row = centre row)
output(r,c,Maxc) :- row(r), col(c), r = CR.

% ensure the bottomright corner also has the colour
output(S-1,S-1,Maxc) :- row(S-1), col(S-1), Maxc.

% any other cell in the square must be zero
:- output(r,c,Color), r != CR, c != CC, neq(Color,0).

% show only the output predicate
#show output/3.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Find the colour that has a cell at its
%     bottomright corner (maxRow , maxCol) and
%     also appears on the top row, bottom row,
%     left column and right column of its bounding box.
% -------------------------------------------------
% helper predicates
rowHasC(R,C) :- input(R,_,C).
colHasC(C,R) :- input(_,C,R).

% extremes for each colour
minR(C)  :- R = #min{R : rowHasC(R,C)}.
maxR(C)  :- R = #max{R : rowHasC(R,C)}.
minC(C)  :- C = #min{C : colHasC(C,C)}.
maxC(C)  :- C = #max{C : colHasC(C,C)}.

% corner must contain the colour
corner(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), R = maxR(Col), C = maxC(Col).

% corner existence forces the colour to be used
:- not corner(maxR(K), maxC(K), K).

% choose the highest colour that satisfies the corner condition
maxc(K) :- K = #max{Col : corner(maxR(Col), maxC(Col), Col), color(Col)}.

% interior dimensions (always at least 1)
intRows(K) :- I = maxR(K) - minR(K) - 1.
intCols(K) :- J = maxC(K) - minC(K) - 1.
intRows(K) :- I = max(I,1).      % guarantee at least 1
intCols(K) :- J = max(J,1).      % guarantee at least 1

% output square size (make it a square)
size(S)    :- S = max(I,J).

% centre the interior rectangle inside the square
padTop(S,I)    :- PT = (S - I) \ 2.          % rows added on top
padLeft(S,J)   :- PL = (S - J) \ 2.          % columns added on left

% centre of the plus shape inside the square
centRow(S,I,PT) :- CR = PT + (I - 1) \ 2.   % centre row index
centCol(S,J,PL)  :- CC = PL + (J - 1) \ 2.   % centre column index

% -------------------------------------------------
% 2.  Build the output
% -------------------------------------------------
% rows and columns of the square
row(r) :- r >= 0, r < S.
col(c) :- c >= 0, c < S.

% vertical line (all rows, column = centre column)
output(r,c,Maxc) :- row(r), col(c), c = CC.

% horizontal line (all columns, row = centre row)
output(r,c,Maxc) :- row(r), col(c), r = CR.

% ensure the bottomright corner also has the colour
output(S-1,S-1,Maxc) :- row(S-1), col(S-1), Maxc.

% any other cell in the square must be zero
:- output(r,c,Color), r != CR, c != CC, neq(Color,0).

% show only the output predicate
#show output/3.

% -------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Find the colour that has a cell at its
%     bottomright corner (maxRow , maxCol) and
%     also appears on the top row, bottom row,
%     left column and right column of its bounding box.
% -------------------------------------------------
% helper predicates
rowHasC(R,C) :- input(R,_,C).
colHasC(C,R) :- input(_,C,R).

% extremes for each colour
minR(C)  :- R = #min{R : rowHasC(R,C)}.
maxR(C)  :- R = #max{R : rowHasC(R,C)}.
minC(C)  :- C = #min{C : colHasC(C,C)}.
maxC(C)  :- C = #max{C : colHasC(C,C)}.

% corner must contain the colour
corner(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), R = maxR(Col), C = maxC(Col).

% corner existence forces the colour to be used
:- not corner(maxR(K), maxC(K), K).

% choose the highest colour that satisfies the corner condition
maxc(K) :- K = #max{Col : corner(maxR(Col), maxC(Col), Col), color(Col)}.

% interior dimensions (always at least 1)
intRows(K) :- I = maxR(K) - minR(K) - 1.
intCols(K) :- J = maxC(K) - minC(K) - 1.
intRows(K) :- I = max(I,1).      % guarantee at least 1
intCols(K) :- J = max(J,1).      % guarantee at least 1

% output square size (make it a square)
size(S)    :- S = max(I,J).

% centre the interior rectangle inside the square
padTop(S,I)    :- PT = (S - I) / 2.          % rows added on top
padLeft(S,J)   :- PL = (S - J) / 2.          % columns added on left

% centre of the plus shape inside the square
centRow(S,I,PT) :- CR = PT + (I - 1) / 2.   % centre row index
centCol(S,J,PL)  :- CC = PL + (J - 1) / 2.   % centre column index

% -------------------------------------------------
% 2.  Build the output
% -------------------------------------------------
% rows and columns of the square
row(r) :- r >= 0, r < S.
col(c) :- c >= 0, c < S.

% vertical line (all rows, column = centre column)
output(r,c,K) :- row(r), col(c), c = CC.

% horizontal line (all columns, row = centre row)
output(r,c,K) :- row(r), col(c), r = CR.

% ensure the bottomright corner also has the colour
output(S-1,S-1,K) :- row(S-1), col(S-1), K.

% any other cell in the square must be zero
:- output(r,c,Color), r != CR, c != CC, neq(Color,0).

% show only the output predicate
#show output/3.
